preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial stop [p] to the bilabial nasal [m] is a process of nasalization. It typically occurs through regressive nasal assimilation, where the stop adopts the lowered velum (nasal airflow) of a following sound.

examples.note: Historical regressive assimilation where the bilabial stop became a nasal before an [m].
Synchronous assimilation in rapid speech where the voiced/voiceless labial stop is nasalized.
